topic: sending money to family in Haiti — Western Union fees

turns.content: let me ask you something because this has been bugging me all week why does western union charge so much to send money to haiti like its actual robbery
It's a really common frustration — remittance fees to the Caribbean and Central America are some of the highest in the world. How much are you seeing in fees right now?

okay so i sent $300 to my aunt in port au prince last tuesday and the fee was like $25 plus the exchange rate they give you is worse than the real rate so by the time it gets there shes probably getting like $260 worth of gourdes out of my $300 thats insane to me

Yeah, the exchange rate markup is the hidden cost that most people don't even notice. The posted fee is bad enough, but when they give you a rate that's 3-5% worse than the mid-market rate, that's another $10-15 disappearing on top of the $25 fee. On $300, you're losing close to $40 total. How often are you sending money?

every month man sometimes twice if something comes up like last month my aunts roof was leaking and she needed extra for the repair and the month before that my cousin needed school fees for his kids and you know when family calls you dont say no especially when youre the one who made it out you know what i mean sa k pase thats just how it is

I totally get that — when you're the one who made it to the US, there's an expectation and a responsibility that comes with it. But if you're sending $300-600 a month and losing $40-80 each time in fees and exchange rate markups, that's close to $500-1000 a year just in transfer costs. Have you looked into any alternatives to Western Union?

i tried zelle but thats only domestic and i looked at paypal but my aunt doesnt have a bank account in haiti she picks up cash from the western union office in port au prince thats the problem you know most of my family there doesnt do digital stuff my aunt is 63 and she walks to the western union and they give her cash and thats how it works

That's the real barrier — cash pickup. Have you looked into Remitly or WorldRemit? Both of them offer cash pickup locations in Haiti, often at banks like Unibank or Sogebank, and the fees are usually lower than Western Union. Some transfers under $500 can be as low as $4-5 in fees, and their exchange rates tend to be better. Your aunt would just go to a different pickup location.

wait really i didnt know about those i been using western union for like 10 years because thats what my mom always used and i just never thought to look at anything else thats probably exactly what they are counting on right like people just keep using them out of habit

Exactly — Western Union's entire business model relies on habit and brand recognition. They were the only option for decades, so whole communities just default to them. But the market has changed a lot. Remitly in particular is popular for Haiti transfers. You can set it up on your phone, pay with your debit card, and your aunt picks up cash at a partner location. The app even lets you track when the money is available for pickup.

on my phone that would actually be perfect for me because half the time im trying to do western union from a truck stop and i have to find one with an actual western union counter inside and sometimes im in the middle of nowhere mississippi and the nearest one is 40 miles away and i have to plan my whole route around it i was listening to a podcast about fintech the other day and they were saying that remittance companies are the biggest scam in financial services and western union was the main example they used

Yeah, there's been a lot of attention on remittance fees. The World Bank tracks them and has been pushing for lower costs for years. The global average is around 6%, but Haiti corridors can be closer to 8-10% when you factor in the exchange rate spread. Being able to do it from your phone would save you the detour hassle too — just send it from wherever you're parked.

okay im going to download remitly right now and try it for the next transfer which is coming up soon because my aunts birthday is in september and i always send extra for that she is the one who took care of me and my sister when my mom was working three jobs in miami after we came from haiti so i owe her everything you know bondye bon for people like her if i can save even $20 a month on fees thats $240 a year and that money should be going to my family not to some corporation

Absolutely — $240 a year back in your aunt's pocket instead of Western Union's is real money, especially in Port-au-Prince. Your aunt sounds like an incredible person. When you set up Remitly, just make sure the cash pickup location is somewhere convenient for her — check which bank or agent is closest to where she lives. And compare the exchange rate they show you against the actual USD to gourde rate to see how much better it is.

yeah ill check the locations near her area for sure and you know its funny i was just thinking that every haitian in america is dealing with this same exact problem right now like theres probably a million of us sending money home every month and getting robbed by the fees and nobody talks about it because its just normal to us like oh yeah i lost $40 to western union again this month thats just life and it shouldnt be you know okay mesi for the help im downloading the app right now before i forget and start driving again happy early birthday to tante lucienne from the both of us ha

Ha, happy birthday Tante Lucienne! You're right — it's one of those things that's so normalized in immigrant communities that people don't question it. But $40 a month adds up to real money that should be going to family, not fees. Hope the app works out for you. Safe driving out there.
